rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model; do not introduce package-qualified names or undefined types.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they describe interface behavior or implementation details.
Do not invent numeric bounds or assumptions not explicitly stated in the requirement or AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible ports, features, and constants from the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Avoid generating clauses that assume numeric bounds or event port values not explicitly defined in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
